name: Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar
region_intro: Mountain biking around Motilla Del Palancar offers diverse terrain within the Cuenca province of Spain. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including the dramatic ravines and cliffs of the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel, carved by the Cabriel River. Riders can expect a mix of tracks, trails, and technical sections through forests and canyons. The area also features routes around the Alarcón reservoir, providing different types of scenery and riding experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Motilla Del Palancar The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar, a 34.8 miles (56.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route circles the Alarcón reservoir, offering open views across the water. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Castillo de Paracuellos por el río Guadazaón – circular desde Campillo de Altobuey, a moderate 30.1 miles (48.5 km) path. This route explores the area around the Guadazaón River and passes by the historic Castillo de Paracuellos. Local mountain bikers also love the View of the Júcar River – Júcar River loop from Barchín del Hoyo, a 68.1 miles (109.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain with views of the Júcar River, often completed in about 8 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Motilla Del Palancar is defined by river valleys, reservoir paths, and the dramatic ravines of Hoces del Cabriel. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Motilla Del Palancar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 25 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Motilla Del Palancar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Motilla Del Palancar?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from tracks and trails to more technical sections. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including the dramatic ravines and cliffs of the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel, carved by the Cabriel River, as well as routes around the Alarcón reservoir. Expect a mix of forests, canyons, and open views across water.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the area does offer options for different ability levels. For a moderate ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017729121/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 56 km long and circles the reservoir, offering open views.

Q: What are some of the longer or more challenging mountain bike routes in the area?
A: For a more challenging and longer ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar River – Júcar River loop from Barchín del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 109.6 km trail that takes nearly 9 hours to complete, leading through varied terrain with views of the Júcar River.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017729121/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017875610/castillo-de-paracuellos-por-el-rio-guadazaon-circular-desde-campillo-de-altobuey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Paracuellos por el río Guadazaón – circular desde Campillo de Altobuey&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lavajo de San Gregorio loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to see the spectacular geography of the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel with its deep ravines and vertical cliffs, as well as the crystal-clear waters of the Cabriel River. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017729121/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; route offers open views of the Alarcón reservoir.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest near the mountain biking routes?
A: Yes, the region blends natural beauty with cultural heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017875610/castillo-de-paracuellos-por-el-rio-guadazaon-circular-desde-campillo-de-altobuey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Paracuellos por el río Guadazaón – circular desde Campillo de Altobuey&lt;/a&gt; route passes by the historic Castillo de Paracuellos. You might also encounter the medieval Vadocañas Bridge within the Hoces del Cabriel Natural Park. Nearby, you can explore the traditional charm of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6062727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valverdejo&lt;/a&gt; or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Loma&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Motilla Del Palancar?
A: The mountain biking routes around Motilla Del Palancar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys and reservoir paths to the dramatic ravines of Hoces del Cabriel, offering options for different ability levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Motilla Del Palancar?
A: Motilla Del Palancar, located in the Cuenca province of Spain, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding, depending on specific weather conditions.

Q: Are there any specific natural parks or protected areas where I can mountain bike?
A: Yes, a primary draw for mountain bikers near Motilla del Palancar is the Natural Park of Hoces del Cabriel. This protected area is renowned for its spectacular geography, carved by the Cabriel River, featuring deep ravines, steep slopes, and vertical cliffs, creating a dramatic and varied landscape ideal for challenging rides.

Q: How many mountain bike routes are available in the Motilla Del Palancar area?
A: There are at least 6 mountain bike routes detailed in this guide for the Motilla Del Palancar area, ranging from moderate to difficult, offering a variety of experiences for riders.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Júcar River?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar River – Júcar River loop from Barchín del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 109.6 km trail that leads through varied terrain with extensive views of the Júcar River.

Q: What is the typical duration for a mountain bike ride in this region?
A: Ride durations vary significantly depending on the route and your pace. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017729121/embalse-de-alarcon-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Alarcón – circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; typically take around 4 hours, while longer, more difficult trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40303676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Júcar River – Júcar River loop from Barchín del Hoyo&lt;/a&gt; can take nearly 9 hours.
tips: A picturesque itinerary that runs along the banks of the Alarcón reservoir with beautiful views. Ideal for the hot months, a bath in its waters will refresh you, the path has no shade, so I recommend you bring sun protection.
From this viewpoint you will have the best views of the gorges of the Júcar river on its way around the town of Alarcón. You will also be able to appreciate the castle of the town, a true postcard.

waypoints.name: Walk along the shore of Alarcón Reservoir
